title: Red Hat Security Advisory: opentelemetry-collector security update
overview:
An update for opentelemetry-collector is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AppStream EUS (v. 10.0)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: opentelemetry-collector security update

An update for opentelemetry-collector is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for opentelemetry-collector is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
opentelemetry-collector-0.135.0-2.el10_0.src.rpm
opentelemetry-collector-0.135.0-2.el10_0.x86_64.rpm
opentelemetry-collector-0.135.0-2.el10_0.s390x.rpm
opentelemetry-collector-0.135.0-2.el10_0.ppc64le.rpm
opentelemetry-collector-0.135.0-2.el10_0.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-68156: A flaw was found in Expr, an expression language and expression evaluation for Go. This vulnerability allows a denial of service (DoS) via recursive traversal over user-provided deeply nested or cyclic data structures without enforcing a maximum recursion depth, leading to a stack overflow panic and application crash.
